mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital for corporations and encourages investment, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat during periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% for more than 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate weaker economic activity.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% is near full employment, making increases more impactful
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE increases by 0.5pp or more over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, suggesting a healthy and expanding economy. This positive sentiment and fundamental strength drive corporate revenues and profits, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average > 1000k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST are currently strong
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous strong RSAFS growth and high HOUST levels are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in over 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of rising inflation suggests stagflationary pressures, where supply constraints limit output while prices rise. This scenario erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat during more than 30% of identified stagflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a strong overall economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence. This fundamental strength supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is currently showing moderate growth
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of quarters following GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeding 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth signifies a tightening labor market and increasing consumer income, which boosts aggregate demand. This positive economic momentum supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high and persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, while also potentially forcing the central bank to tighten monetary policy. This combination creates headwinds for equity markets, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation > 3.0% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is currently moderate, but a rise would be concerning
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates a significant weakening in the construction sector and broader economic confidence, often preceding a general economic slowdown. This reduction in future economic activity translates to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST is currently strong, so a fall would be a significant negative signal
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods when HOUST (3-month average) drops below 800k units.
